choice: Store birthdate, planned retirement age, and per-person plan-end age defaulting to 105 (matching the spreadsheet's age-105 horizon); keep Social Security / pension as income streams linked to the person, not raw person fields.
rationale: The spreadsheet runs a two-person household from age 50 (2024) to 105 (2079), so age, retirement age, and a mortality horizon are load-bearing. Deriving age from birthdate (vs. storing a static age like the sheet does) is a deliberate delta — it keeps daily-valuation projections correct as real time passes, which the tracking objective requires. Comparable tools (ProjectionLab, BoldIn) model retirement age and longevity per person.
